{{Quote|Ree Duptom—he's the only one who ever got booted ''out'' of the Bounty Hunters Guild!|[[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]]|<ref name="Hard Merchandise" />}}
'''Ree Duptom''' was a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]—a notably unpopular one—during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the ''[[Venesectrix]]''. He was apparently the only bounty hunter kicked out of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] for falsely incriminating a pair of fellow guildsmen and profiting from their misfortunes. Others in the trade considered Duptom the lowest of scum, but it didn't stop him from being an independent hunter. Duptom used the same unethical method under [[Kuat (person)|Kuat]] of [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat's]] payroll to make it seem as though [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] was behind the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] raid on the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars]] [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].

Some [[Time/Legends|time]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Duptom was found [[Death/Legends|dead]] by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], having perished from [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] exposure to the ''Venesectrix'' engines. In one of the prisoner cages aboard the ship, Fett discovered that Duptom had been keeping a recently [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory-wiped]] [[Kateel of Kuhlvult|Neelah]] and brought her aboard [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']] for safe-keeping, along with the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] used to record the false evidence of the moisture farm raid. There was no hard evidence to determine whether Duptom died of his own carelessness in maintaining his engines or if it was purposeful sabotage. It was known that he had many enemies.

==Behind the scenes==
Ree Duptom is an anagram of [[Tom Dupree]], an editor at [[Bantam Spectra]] where ''[[Hard Merchandise]]'' was published.
